hero.title: AI Processing Platform
hero.subtitle: A 3-phase evolutionary architecture for scalable AI task processing

architecture.title: Architecture Evolution
architecture.subtitle: From MVP to Scale — infrastructure grows with business demand
architecture.throughputHelpPhase1: Target envelope for MVP, not a hard AWS limit. Sized for low-ops validation with managed STT/LLM and modest worker concurrency.
architecture.throughputHelpPhase2: Based on STT as the bottleneck. 1 A10G STT GPU delivers about 10-12 tasks/min, so a practical Growth setup with 2-4 STT GPUs yields about 20-50 tasks/min sustained.
architecture.throughputHelpPhase3: Also STT-bound. Roughly 20 STT GPUs deliver about 200-240 tasks/min, and about 100 STT GPUs deliver about 1,000-1,200 tasks/min, so Scale is shown as a broad capacity range.

taskFlow.title: Task Processing Flow
taskFlow.subtitle: From audio upload to summarized result — fully async pipeline

capacity.title: Capacity Planner
capacity.subtitle: Calculate GPU requirements and costs for your workload
capacity.plannerGuideBody: Monthly volume is how many tasks you process per month. Audio length affects STT time directly. Whisper RTF means processing time relative to audio duration: lower is faster. Input and output tokens drive managed LLM cost. GPU utilization is your target headroom, not a performance boost.
capacity.priceDriversBody: Managed cost is driven mostly by audio minutes and Bedrock tokens. Self-hosted cost is driven mostly by GPU count, which depends on STT bottleneck, LLM throughput, and your utilization target. Model choice changes per-token pricing, while RTF and throughput settings mainly change how many GPUs you need.
capacity.gpuUtilizationHint: Controls GPU over-provisioning ratio. Below ~100K tasks/mo only 1 GPU is needed — changing this has no cost impact.
capacity.llmThroughputHint: Estimated vLLM continuous batching throughput. Depends on model size and prompt length. Below ~100K tasks/mo only 1 GPU is needed — changing this has no cost impact.

cost.title: Cost Crossover Analysis
cost.subtitle: When does self-hosting GPU become cheaper than managed services?
cost.chartGuideBody: The chart compares managed AI cost and self-hosted GPU cost as task volume grows. Managed pricing rises almost linearly with usage, while self-hosted starts higher but grows more slowly after the fixed GPU baseline is covered.
cost.crossoverGuideBody: The crossover point is where self-hosted becomes cheaper than managed service for the same workload. Left of that line, managed is usually simpler and cheaper. Right of that line, GPU self-hosting usually becomes the more cost-efficient choice.
